patches.value: Registry-policy aware: never write immutable or scan-derived registry fields via patch intent; require post-move scan/recompute to refresh recompute_on_scan fields.
Protect directory identity anchors: .dir_id artifacts must remain co-located with their directory; never move .dir_id independently.
Identity resolution uses the standard resolver chain (path match -> rename intent -> strong identity -> orphan/conflict). Malformed ID-like prefixes are not treated as gaps.

value.name: 00_IDENTITY_RESOLUTION_REPORT.csv
value.purpose: Per-item identity resolution outcome using the resolver chain: resolved_by, file_id_candidate, prefix_validity, conflict_class, and recommended action (keep/quarantine/review).

value.name: 00_PLANNED_VS_PRESENT_RECONCILIATION.csv
value.purpose: Registry PLANNED entries missing on disk, disk-present files missing in registry, and conflicts/orphans; used to prevent silent drift.

value.name: 00_POST_MOVE_SCAN_QUEUE.csv
value.purpose: List of file_ids/paths requiring scan/recompute refresh after execution; consumed by reconciler/watchers.

value.name: 00_WRITE_POLICY_VALIDATION_REPORT.md
value.purpose: Validation result of any generated patch intent against the registry write policy (immutable/recompute/manual_patch_only).

value.name: Identity Resolution (Gov_Reg Chain)
value.objective: Resolve stable identity for each inventory row using the standard resolver chain and classify malformed/duplicate ID-like prefixes.
actions: If IDENTITY_RESOLUTION_MODE=govreg_chain: resolve identity using chain: path_exact -> rename_intent -> strong_identity_prefix -> orphan_or_conflict.
Validate any detected 20-digit prefixes; classify malformed prefixes as prefix_invalid (recommended action quarantine/review).
Detect duplicate prefix collisions and mark as conflict.
Emit 00_IDENTITY_RESOLUTION_REPORT.csv and enrich 00_INVENTORY.csv with identity_resolution + prefix_validity + file_id (when resolved).
acceptance: Every inventory row has identity_resolution outcome
Malformed prefix cases are not labeled as 'missing id'

value: For each group in GROUPS in order: filter inventory using GROUP_FILTERS[group].
If REGISTRY_ASSISTED_CLASSIFICATION=true: enrich each row with registry lifecycle_state + geu_role + artifact_kind (when available).
If LIFECYCLE_AWARE_CLASSIFICATION=true: apply lifecycle precedence rules before extension/path heuristics (e.g., DEPRECATED -> archive/export default; CONFLICT/ORPHAN -> quarantine/review default).
Apply classification heuristics: extension, folder hints, naming patterns, reference scanning (optional), authority rules.
If ACTIVE_ONLY_POLICY=true: default non-active to export unless explicitly kept or needs_review.
If identity_resolution indicates prefix_invalid or conflict: default to quarantine/review; never auto-assign IDs for these rows.
Mark uncertain items as needs_review and default to keep (fail-safe).
Generate group Move Map subset (either separate file per group or one combined map with group column).
If PAPER_MOVE_MAP_ONLY=true: also generate 00_PAPER_MOVE_MAP.csv with proposed final destinations (no execution).
If registry entries exist in PLANNED state but missing on disk: emit 00_PLANNED_VS_PRESENT_RECONCILIATION.csv rows.
For each group in order: validate all src exist and are within allowed scope.

If PROTECT_DIR_ID_FILES=true: detect any Move Map row whose src_rel matches DIR_ID_FILE_NAMES or whose dst_rel would relocate a dir_id anchor independently; block execution for that group and emit conflicts.
Verify destination rules: by default dst must be within TARGET_DIR; if ALLOW_CROSS_ROOT_MOVES=true then dst may be in allowlisted RELATED_DIRS.
Detect collisions: case-insensitive duplicates, existing dst, path-length overflow.
Write 00_CONFLICTS.csv; if conflicts exist, halt unless override rules exist.
If PAPER_MOVE_MAP_ONLY=true: do not modify filesystem; write an execution log event indicating 'paper_only'.
Else if DRY_RUN=true: simulate actions; write logs; do not modify filesystem.
Else: for each group in stable order: for each Move Map row in stable order: perform move/export/keep/quarantine.
Preserve relative path when exporting; avoid overwrites; on conflict skip + log.
If any move/export executed and POST_MOVE_SCAN_REQUIRED=true: emit 00_POST_MOVE_SCAN_QUEUE.csv listing affected file_ids/paths.
If INVENTORY_REFRESH_PER_GROUP=true and not DRY_RUN and not PAPER_MOVE_MAP_ONLY: re-run inventory (T01) after each group completes.
If HASH_MODE=sha256 and DRY_RUN=false: spot-check or full-check moved file hashes where feasible.
If FINALIZATION_MODE=final: run DIR_TYPE checks (code: import/test smoke; docs: link check; schema: schema validation if configured).
If FINALIZATION_MODE=staging: skip mandatory reference rewrite checks and skip registry/ledger commitments; record as deferred in report.

If POST_MOVE_SCAN_REQUIRED=true and any moves executed: require that post-move scan/recompute was run (or is explicitly queued with a consumed queue marker); otherwise fail verification as 'registry_drift_risk'.
If ID_MODE=enforce_and_rename: perform file_id and dir_id renames as planned; update Move Map and logs.
Perform reference rewrites (imports/includes/manifests/CI) as required and log changes.
If REGISTRY_MODE=full: perform required registry/ledger updates (reservations/commitments) and record evidence.

If WRITE_POLICY_ENFORCEMENT in {validate_patch_intent_only, validate_and_block}: validate any generated patch intent against the registry write policy and exclude immutable/never-writable/recompute_on_scan fields; emit 00_WRITE_POLICY_VALIDATION_REPORT.md.
Re-run verification checks with final mode requirements.
